assumptions_to_verify: Whether production Codex CLI can return a valid typed outcome without executing a command; the code permits this case.
Whether the desired non-agentic fallback should be text_only or self_reported; the task text says otherwise text_only.
Whether full suite stays green after fixing assurance mapping and adding the missing regression.
contradictions_checked: Cursor SDK route evidence failed with reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable while Codex CLI route evidence returned a typed read-only verdict.
Panel wiring loops over configured_reviewers and evaluator receives the full reviewer result list.
Outage handling keeps reviewer-1 missing as panel decision=revise and records degraded recovery metadata.
Claude's claim that agentic metadata is justified by tools and hashes is contradicted by the no-command codex_cli mapping path.
missing_evidence: Regression proving a Codex CLI typed outcome with zero command executions is not exported as agentic.
Independent pytest execution from this review environment; I relied on receipts and static inspection.
A live rigorous run recording two real successful reviewer verdicts simultaneously.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The implementation violates P2/non-goal provenance truthfulness: CodexCliReviewer correctly downgrades reviewer_assurance to self_reported when no command_executions exist, but _assurance_grade still returns agentic for any codex_cli runtime. A prompt-only typed Codex verdict can therefore be exported as assurance_grade=agentic without the required tool evidence.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Fix assurance mapping so agentic requires tool_backed_primary or command evidence, add a no-command Codex CLI regression, rerun focused and full suites, and provide live two-verdict evidence or explicitly defer that evidence to outcome_review without claiming it is already satisfied.

claims: Second reviewer is openai/codex_cli/gpt-5.5, distinct family from Claude lead and Gemini reviewer
Agentic grade justified by tool access + read-only sandbox + transcript refs + stdout/stderr hashes
Conservative rules unchanged and applied across both reviewers (non-weighted)
Missing reviewer verdict degrades honestly (revise/missing_reviewer_verdict; not counted as accept) per P4 and stdio:4122 guard
Route blocking-question resolved by real codex-cli-readonly probe evidence
confidence_criteria: Plan file content read directly and is sound/complete/traceable
All 5 P1-P5 tests exist at named lines
git diff --stat confirms 919 ins/4 files realized
CodexCliReviewer agentic metadata + read-only sandbox + hashing verified in source
Recovery guard skips missing reviewers (never counts accept)
Route evidence shows real typed agentic accept verdict
outcome.confidence_rationale: All planning + realization claims verified by direct file reads, grep of named tests, git diff --stat, and route-evidence inspection. Not 0.95 because pytest was not executed (no approval) and acceptance #1's live two-verdict run was not demonstrated (degraded replay only) — both are OUTCOME-gate obligations, not implementation_plan defects.

assumptions_to_verify: Live codex CLI route returns a typed verdict under load in the real workflow (OUTCOME gate)
Full pytest suite is green including the new panel tests
contradictions_checked: independent-reviewer-1 missing verdict is honest-degradation by design (P4 + guard), not a regression or silent accept
reviewer-1 shares family with Codex supervisor but is still distinct from BOTH correlated lineages and is disclosed in plan Risk L49-52
Non-goals respected: panel is non-weighted, agentic grade gated on tools+hashes, conservative rules unchanged
missing_evidence: A live rigorous run recording two real cross-family verdicts simultaneously
pytest pass/fail output for the full suite (not run; approval not granted)
Independently re-derived sha256 of implementation-plan.md (shasum approval denied)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Acceptance #1 requires TWO real LIVE independent verdicts in a rigorous run, but only an isolated route probe + deterministic fakes are demonstrated; the captured replay run was degraded with reviewer-1 missing.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the plan failed to register a distinct-family reviewer, mislabeled a text-only reviewer as agentic, changed the conservative rules, or any named P1-P5 test were absent — none of which hold.

outcome.summary: implementation_plan gate ACCEPT. Plan is sound/complete/traceable and realized in tree (919 ins/4 files). reviewer_registry.py registers reviewer-1 as openai/codex_cli/gpt-5.5 with agentic grade justified by tools+read-only sandbox+transcript+hashes, a distinct family from both Claude lead and Gemini reviewer. Conservative non-weighted aggregator applies across both; missing verdict yields revise/missing_reviewer_verdict and the recovery guard never counts a missing reviewer as accept. Route blocking-question resolved by real codex-cli-readonly probe evidence (accept verdict, command observed, transcript hash). P1-P5 tests all exist. The independent-reviewer-1 corrective objection is the by-design honest-degradation path proven by P4, not a planning defect.
